documentation.compatibilityDesc: Our API is fully compatible with OpenAI's interface standards. You can directly use the official OpenAI SDK or any third-party libraries without modifying your code logic, just replace the BASE URL and API KEY. We support all OpenAI interfaces, including but not limited to chat completions, embeddings, image generation, etc.
documentation.faq1Answer: You can view the real-time credit balance in the platform console's "Account Center - Credit Management" module.
documentation.faq2Answer: The platform promises 99% model availability, ensuring service stability through multi-node disaster recovery and intelligent scheduling.
documentation.faq3Answer: Simply specify the target model in the model parameter (e.g., use doubao-seed-1.6 when calling Doubao model), and the platform will automatically route to the corresponding model service provider.
documentation.faq3Question: How to switch between different vendors' models?
documentation.faq4Answer:
It's very simple! You only need to modify two parameters:
1) Change the BASE URL to the address we provide;
2) Use the API KEY created on this platform to replace the original key.
No other code changes are required.
documentation.faq4Question: How to migrate from OpenAI to this platform?
documentation.faq5Answer:
We support 30+ series and 500+ models, including:
• International models: GPT-5, Claude 4.5, Gemini 3 Pro, etc.
• Chinese models: Qwen Qwen3, GLM 4.5, Kimi K2, MiniMax M2, etc.
You can get the complete model list through the /v1/models endpoint.
documentation.faq6Answer: We use a credit-based billing system. You can top up credits anytime, and credits can be used for any model. The prices are more competitive, and you don't need to worry about billing differences between different providers.
documentation.modelsListApiDesc: Use a GET request to the /v1/models endpoint to retrieve a list of all available models enabled for your account. The request must include a valid API Key in the Header.
documentation.modelsListDesc: The platform supports 30+ model series and 500+ available models. The following are typical model examples (for the complete real-time list, please use the /v1/models endpoint):
documentation.modelsListFieldObject: Object type, model for text models, image for image models
documentation.modelsListResponseDesc: The endpoint returns a JSON-formatted list of models, with each model containing the following fields:
documentation.modelsOtherDesc: Covering 30+ model series with 500+ available models
documentation.step1Desc: Use the following BASE URL to replace OpenAI's official address
documentation.step2Desc: Go to the API Key page to create your exclusive key
documentation.step3Desc: Use your familiar OpenAI SDK, just replace BASE URL and API KEY
documentation.streamDesc: Use streaming responses to achieve a typewriter effect, allowing users to see generated content in real-time. Simply set stream=True in your request to enable it.
documentation.subtitle: Learn how to quickly integrate our API service, fully compatible with OpenAI interface standards
documentation.thinkingBestPractice1: Simple tasks (no thinking needed): For simple requests that don't require complex reasoning (such as fact retrieval or classification), thinking is not needed.
documentation.thinkingBestPractice2: Medium tasks (default/requires some thinking): Many common requests require some degree of step-by-step processing or deeper understanding, and Gemini can flexibly use thinking capabilities to handle them.
documentation.thinkingBestPractice3: Difficult tasks (maximum thinking capability): For truly complex challenges, such as solving complex math problems or coding tasks, we recommend setting a higher thinking budget.
documentation.thinkingBestPracticesDesc: Here are some recommendations for efficiently using thinking models:
documentation.thinkingBudgetDesc: Thinking budget, controls the number of thinking tokens the model can use. Set to -1 for unlimited thinking budget. Depending on the prompt, the model may exceed or fall below the token budget.
documentation.thinkingDesc: Gemini 3 and 2.5 series models use internal "thinking processes" that significantly improve reasoning and multi-step planning capabilities, making them ideal for complex tasks such as coding, advanced mathematics, and data analysis.
documentation.thinkingHowDesc: Making requests with thinking models is similar to making any other content generation request. The main difference is specifying a model that supports thinking capabilities (such as gemini-3-pro-preview, gemini-2.5-pro, etc.) in the model field.
documentation.thinkingLevelDesc: Thinking depth level, options: low, medium, high.
documentation.thinkingNoteDesc: The Gemini 3 Pro Preview model does not support disabling thinking. This model will always perform thinking processes.
documentation.thinkingParamsDesc: The thinking parameter contains two fields:
documentation.thinkingPricingDesc: When thinking features are enabled, the answer price is the sum of output tokens and thinking tokens. Thinking models generate complete thoughts to improve the quality of the final answer, then output summaries, so pricing is based on the full number of thinking tokens required to generate the summary.
documentation.thinkingSummaryDesc: Thought summary is a synthesized version of the model's original thinking that helps you gain insights into the model's internal reasoning process. You can enable thought summary by setting the includeThoughts parameter.
documentation.title: API Documentation
documentation.usageDesc: Each API response includes a usage field that details the token consumption for that request, making it easy to control costs and analyze usage patterns.
documentation.usageNoteDesc: Token billing rates vary by model. Input tokens (prompt_tokens) and output tokens (completion_tokens) may have different prices. We recommend checking the specific pricing information for each model before use.

home.betterPrice: Better Prices, Better Uptime, Top up Anytime
home.buyPointsDesc: Credits can be used with any model or provider.
home.createAPIKeyDesc: Create a key and send a request, compatible with OpenAI.
home.createAccountDesc: Create an account to get started.
